Under s 12 of the MDA, a person who “attempts to commit … any offence … shall be guilty of that offence and shall be liable on conviction to the punishment provided for that offence”. This provision deems a person who attempted an offence as having committed that offence, and makes him liable to be punished as if he had committed that offence (see Ali bin Mohamad Bahashwan v Public Prosecutor [2018] 1 SLR 610 at [38]).
